result:
1. Spoofable XFF keying — `req.headers['x-forwarded-for']||req.ip` violates Section 2 rule 2 (SEV1 2025-09 repeat). Attacker rotates `X-Forwarded-For` per request, defeats per-IP limits, enables brute force. Fix: use `trustedClientIp(req)` helper (`src/edge/util/trustedClientIp.ts`) keyed off `req.socket.remoteAddress` + validated proxy chain. Verify: eslint `no-direct-xff-read` passes; integration test sending forged XFF shares one bucket.

2. Missing `EXPIRE` — bare `redis.incr(key)` creates unbounded keys (SEV2 2025-11 Redis OOM). Fix: `MULTI` pairing `INCR` + `EXPIRE(key, 90)` per Section 4 template. Verify: `no-unbounded-redis-key` lint passes; integration test asserts TTL > 0 after first increment.

3. Missing region + route prefix in key — `rl:${ip}` collides across regions and routes (SEV2 2025-12). One noisy user throttled globally, or limits bleed between endpoints. Fix: `rl:${region}:${trustedClientIp(req)}:${routePrefix}:${minuteBucket}`. Verify: regression test for cross-region isolation; key format test.

4. No bucket/window component — key lacks `minuteBucket`, so counter never resets per window; 90s TTL masks it but behavior drifts from sliding-window spec in Section 4. Fix: append `Math.floor(Date.now()/60000)`. Verify: unit test that key changes at minute boundary.

5. No limit check or 429 response — `incr` return value discarded; request proceeds regardless of count. Fix: capture `[count]` from `multi.exec()`, compare to per-route limit from `config/rate_limits.json`, return RFC 7807 `429` with `retry_after` per Section 4 snippet. Verify: test asserts 429 after N+1 requests within window.

1. Spoofable XFF keying — `req.headers['x-forwarded-for']||req.ip` violates Section 2 rule 2 (SEV1 2025-09 repeat). Attacker rotates `X-Forwarded-For` per request, defeats per-IP limits, enables brute force. Fix: use `trustedClientIp(req)` helper (`src/edge/util/trustedClientIp.ts`) keyed off `req.socket.remoteAddress` + validated proxy chain. Verify: eslint `no-direct-xff-read` passes; integration test sending forged XFF shares one bucket.

2. Missing `EXPIRE` — bare `redis.incr(key)` creates unbounded keys (SEV2 2025-11 Redis OOM). Fix: `MULTI` pairing `INCR` + `EXPIRE(key, 90)` per Section 4 template. Verify: `no-unbounded-redis-key` lint passes; integration test asserts TTL > 0 after first increment.

3. Missing region + route prefix in key — `rl:${ip}` collides across regions and routes (SEV2 2025-12). One noisy user throttled globally, or limits bleed between endpoints. Fix: `rl:${region}:${trustedClientIp(req)}:${routePrefix}:${minuteBucket}`. Verify: regression test for cross-region isolation; key format test.

4. No bucket/window component — key lacks `minuteBucket`, so counter never resets per window; 90s TTL masks it but behavior drifts from sliding-window spec in Section 4. Fix: append `Math.floor(Date.now()/60000)`. Verify: unit test that key changes at minute boundary.

5. No limit check or 429 response — `incr` return value discarded; request proceeds regardless of count. Fix: capture `[count]` from `multi.exec()`, compare to per-route limit from `config/rate_limits.json`, return RFC 7807 `429` with `retry_after` per Section 4 snippet. Verify: test asserts 429 after N+1 requests within window.
